dialog: Hmm. I see you're progressing, Neura. Good.
Oh! Another teacher! And you look... tilted. Are you okay?
I'm perfectly fine. I'm just... inclined. But there's a problem you'll eventually encounter: data doesn't always pass through the center.
Not everything in life starts at zero. Sometimes you need a... shift. A correction. A Bias.
Bias? Like... being unfair? That sounds bad!
No, no. Not that kind of bias. Imagine a scale that always reads 2 kg more than what's actually there. That scale has a bias.
In machine learning it's the same: bias is a value we add to shift the prediction in the right direction, regardless of inputs.
So it's like an adjustment? An offset? That actually makes sense!
